title: Order Settlement
summary: Cross-context domain model example centered on command, event, and query flow across orders and payments.

semantic.context.id: The bounded context currently shown as the business ownership boundary.
semantic.context.owners: Teams, roles, or organizations that carry business responsibility for this context.
semantic.context.responsibilities: The business responsibilities explicitly assigned to this context.
semantic.context.owned_aggregates: Aggregates modeled inside this context boundary.
semantic.context.scenarios: Scenarios on the default learning path for this context.
semantic.context.related_actors: Actors that initiate or participate in steps inside this context.
semantic.context.related_systems: Systems this context collaborates with, depends on, or calls.
semantic.context.relationships: External collaborations or dependencies explicitly declared for this context.
semantic.actor.type: The participant type for this actor, such as person, role, or team.
semantic.actor.contexts: Contexts where this actor participates in at least one step.
semantic.actor.scenario_steps: Scenario steps where this actor explicitly participates.
semantic.system.boundary: Whether this system is modeled as an internal capability or an external dependency.
semantic.system.capabilities: Capabilities this system explicitly provides to the business model.
semantic.system.contexts: Contexts that depend on this system or collaborate with it through messages.
semantic.system.dependencies: Which contexts, messages, or policies refer to this system and how.
semantic.scenario.id: The end-to-end business scenario used as the default reading storyline.
semantic.scenario.goal: The business goal this scenario tries to achieve.
semantic.scenario.owner_context: The context that owns the main narrative and responsibility for this scenario.
semantic.scenario.participating_contexts: Contexts touched while this scenario advances.
semantic.scenario.actors: Actors that appear in this scenario.
semantic.scenario.systems: Systems that appear in this scenario.
semantic.scenario.final_steps: Final steps where this scenario can conclude.
semantic.step.id: A single business step inside the scenario story.
semantic.step.context: The business context that owns this step.
semantic.step.actor: The actor that initiates or leads this step.
semantic.step.system: The system directly touched or invoked by this step.
semantic.step.entry: Whether this step is marked as the scenario entry point.
semantic.step.final: Whether this step is a final business outcome instead of ongoing work.
semantic.step.incoming_messages: Messages this step receives or observes.
semantic.step.outgoing_messages: Messages this step sends, triggers, or requests.
semantic.step.outcome: A short description of the business result when the scenario ends at this step.
semantic.message.kind: The message category, such as command, event, or query.
semantic.message.type: The business message identifier shown in this view.
semantic.message.channel: Whether the message is expected to travel through sync or async delivery.
semantic.message.endpoints: The source, target, and their owning contexts for this message.
semantic.message.crosses_context_boundary: Whether this message crosses more than one business context.
semantic.message.step_links: Scenario steps linked to this message as incoming or outgoing references.
semantic.message.payload_fields: Payload fields defined on this message contract and the meaning of each field.
semantic.aggregate.id: The aggregate that owns this node or relation.
semantic.aggregate.context: The business context that owns this aggregate lifecycle.
semantic.aggregate.initial_state: The default state at the beginning of the aggregate lifecycle.
semantic.aggregate.lifecycle: All lifecycle states defined for this aggregate.
semantic.aggregate.accepted_messages: Messages that drive transitions in this aggregate lifecycle.
semantic.aggregate.emitted_messages: Messages this aggregate emits during lifecycle transitions.
semantic.aggregate.reachable_states: States that can be reached legally from the initial state.
semantic.aggregate.unreachable_states: States that remain unreachable in the current lifecycle.
semantic.aggregate.state.id: The lifecycle state currently being shown.
semantic.aggregate.state.reachable: Whether this state can be reached from the initial state.
semantic.aggregate.state.outgoing_messages: Messages emitted by transitions that leave this state.
semantic.transition.trigger_message: The message that triggers this lifecycle transition.
semantic.transition.emitted_messages: Additional messages emitted by this lifecycle transition.
semantic.relation.from: The source state, source step, or source node for this relation.
semantic.relation.to: The target state, target step, or target node for this relation.
semantic.policy.id: The identifier of the current coordination policy or saga.
semantic.policy.context: The business context where this policy is declared or owned.
semantic.policy.emitted_messages: Messages this policy emits as follow-up work.
semantic.policy.target_systems: Systems this policy calls or coordinates.
semantic.policy.related_contexts: Contexts this policy touches or coordinates.
semantic.policy.coordinates: Business resources explicitly coordinated by this policy.

views.title: Context Map
views.description: Shows bounded contexts, the actors and systems they collaborate with, and which scenarios, aggregates, and policies each context owns.
